columns.column_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1
columns.extraction_plan: The treatment arm 1 is "SOC + AAP" (Standard-of-care plus Abiraterone Acetate and Prednisolone). The experimental agent added is Abiraterone Acetate. On page 10, in the "DISCUSSION" section (Chunk 7), the text states: "While those agents [apalutamide and enzalutamide] are androgen receptor antagonists, all three drugs [apalutamide, enzalutamide, and abiraterone] work by targeting the androgen receptor axis." The conclusion on the same page also mentions "upfront targeting of the androgen receptor pathway using abiraterone acetate". Therefore, the class of agent for abiraterone acetate is an androgen receptor axis targeting agent.

However, this is not the median PFS.

The primary endpoint is "overall survival". This is explicitly stated in the abstract on page 3, in the sentence: "The primary outcome measure was overall survival." It is also reiterated on page 4, in the "Outcome measures and statistical analysis" section (2.4), in the sentence: "The primary outcome for this comparison was overall survival, with secondary outcomes of failure-free survival, progression-free survival, metastatic progression-free survival, skeletal-related events, disease-specific survival, toxicity and therapy for progression."

columns.column_name: Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: The number and percentage of patients experiencing all-cause adverse events with a grade of 3 or higher in the treatment arm (SOC + AAP) is reported as 80 (16%). This is derived from the text on page 6, "Toxicity at 4 years postrandomisation was similar, with 16% patients in each group reporting grade 3 or higher toxicity." The total number of patients in the SOC + AAP group is 501 (Table 1, page 5), so 16% of 501 is approximately 80 patients. While "all-cause" is not explicitly stated, "toxicity" in this general context typically refers to all-cause.

The "Outcome measures and statistical analysis" section (2.4) on page 4 does not list Quality of Life as a primary or secondary outcome for this specific analysis. The secondary endpoints are explicitly stated in the "Outcome measures and statistical analysis" section (Section 2.4) on page 4, and also listed in Table 2 on page 6.

columns.extraction_plan: In Figure 1, locate the box "Allocated to SOC+AAP" and extract the 'N' value. In Table 1, locate the column header "SOC + AAP" and extract the 'n' value directly below it. Both sources confirm the number of participants in the SOC + AAP arm is 501.

The regimen for Treatment Arm 1 (the experimental arm) is reported in the abstract and the methods section. Extract the regimen described for the "SOC + AAP" group. The abstract states: "SOC-alone with or without daily abiraterone acetate 1000 mg + prednisolone 5 mg (SOC + AAP)". Also, extract the regimen described for the "SOC + AAP" group. It states: "Patients allocated to the SOC + AAP group were also planned to receive abiraterone (1000 mg daily) with prednisolone (5 mg daily)".

The experimental treatment arm is "Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone (AAP)". This is explicitly stated in the abstract on page 2: "Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone (AAP) previously demonstrated improved survival in STAMPEDE". Further details are provided on page 3 in the abstract: "abiraterone acetate 1000 mg + prednisolone 5 mg (SOC + AAP)".

The trial name is "STAMPEDE". It is explicitly mentioned in the title of the article on page 1: "STAMPEDE randomised trial (NCT00268476)". It is also frequently referenced throughout the text, for example, in the abstract on page 3: "Initial results from the STAMPEDE trial for advanced prostate cancer showed that adding abiraterone acetate and prednisone (AAP) to androgen deprivation therapy improved progression-free survival."

The specific outcome measure "Time To Progression of Serum Androgen" (TTPSA) is not reported in the PDF. The document reports "Progression-free survival" (PFS) which is a broader composite endpoint that includes PSA progression, but also radiological or clinical progression. A value specifically for "Time To Progression of Serum Androgen" is not provided for the treatment arm (SOC + AAP).

Extract the treatment arms described in the "Abstract" and "METHODS" sections. The abstract states: "Standard-of-care (SOC) was androgen deprivation therapy." and "The comparison randomised patients 1:1 to SOC-alone with or without daily abiraterone acetate 1000 mg + prednisolone 5 mg (SOC + AAP), continued until disease progression." Value: Standard-of-care (SOC) / androgen deprivation therapy (ADT); Standard-of-care (SOC) + Abiraterone acetate + Prednisolone (AAP)

On page 3, in the Abstract section, extract the sentence "Abiraterone acetate plus prednisolone (AAP) previously demonstrated improved survival in STAMPEDE, a multiarm, multistage platform trial in men starting long-term hormone therapy for prostate cancer." and "Standard-of-care (SOC) was androgen deprivation therapy." Also, in the "2.3 Procedures" section, extract "All patients received lifelong ADT using gonadotrophin-releasing hormone (GnRH) agonists, antagonists, or orchidectomy. Patients allocated to the SOC + AAP group were also planned to receive abiraterone (1000 mg daily) with prednisolone (5 mg daily)."

The 5-year overall survival rate for the SOC + AAP (treatment) group is reported as 60% (95% CI: 55-64%) in Table 2, under the "Overall survival" section, row "% alive at 5 years (95% CI)". This is also mentioned in the text on page 3: "with 5-years survival improved from 41% SOC-alone to 60% SOC + AAP."

The 5-year overall survival rate for the SOC-alone (control) group is reported as 41% (95% CI: 37-45%) in Table 2, under the "Overall survival" section, row "% alive at 5 years (95% CI)". This is also mentioned in the text on page 3: "with 5-years survival improved from 41% SOC-alone to 60% SOC + AAP."

The PDF provides baseline counts for "Newly diagnosed" (which corresponds to synchronous metastasis) patients in Table 1 (941 patients overall, 475 in SOC-alone, 466 in SOC + AAP). However, specific overall survival rate percentages for this subgroup by treatment arm are not reported. The text on page 10 states, "Most patients in STAMPEDE had de novo metastatic disease... Therefore, these trial data could not be used to explore whether there is a differential treat- ment effect by these characteristics."

The PDF provides baseline counts for "Newly diagnosed" (which corresponds to synchronous metastasis) patients in Table 1 (941 patients overall, 475 in SOC-alone, 466 in SOC + AAP). However, specific overall survival rate percentages for this subgroup by treatment arm are not reported. The text on page 10 states, "Most patients in STAMPEDE had de novo metastatic disease... Therefore, these trial data could not be used to explore whether there is a differential treat- ment effect by these characteristics."

The PDF provides baseline counts for "Relapsing" (which corresponds to metachronous metastasis) patients in Table 1 (62 patients overall, 27 in SOC-alone, 35 in SOC + AAP). However, specific overall survival rate percentages for this subgroup by treatment arm are not reported.

The PDF provides baseline counts for "Relapsing" (which corresponds to metachronous metastasis) patients in Table 1 (62 patients overall, 27 in SOC-alone, 35 in SOC + AAP). However, specific overall survival rate percentages for this subgroup by treatment arm are not reported.

The PDF reports overall survival rates for "High-risk" metastatic disease patients, classified using LATITUDE criteria (defined as ≥3 bone metastases; visceral metastases; Gleason score ≥8). The text on page 8 mentions that the LATITUDE and CHAARTED systems for defining metastatic disease risk group largely coincided. The 5-year overall survival rate for the SOC + AAP (treatment) group in the "High-risk" category is reported as 49% (95% CI: 43-55%) in Table 3, under the "Overall survival" section, row "% alive at 5 years, (95% CI)".

The PDF reports overall survival rates for "High-risk" metastatic disease patients, classified using LATITUDE criteria (defined as ≥3 bone metastases; visceral metastases; Gleason score ≥8). The text on page 8 mentions that the LATITUDE and CHAARTED systems for defining metastatic disease risk group largely coincided. The 5-year overall survival rate for the SOC-alone (control) group in the "High-risk" category is reported as 28% (95% CI: 22-34%) in Table 3, under the "Overall survival" section, row "% alive at 5 years, (95% CI)".

The PDF reports overall survival rates for "Low-risk" metastatic disease patients, classified using LATITUDE criteria. The text on page 8 mentions that the LATITUDE and CHAARTED systems for defining metastatic disease risk group largely coincided. The 5-year overall survival rate for the SOC + AAP (treatment) group in the "Low-risk" category is reported as 72% (95% CI: 65-77%) in Table 3, under the "Overall survival" section, row "% alive at 5 years, (95% CI)".

The PDF reports overall survival rates for "Low-risk" metastatic disease patients, classified using LATITUDE criteria. The text on page 8 mentions that the LATITUDE and CHAARTED systems for defining metastatic disease risk group largely coincided. The 5-year overall survival rate for the SOC-alone (control) group in the "Low-risk" category is reported as 55% (95% CI: 48-61%) in Table 3, under the "Overall survival" section, row "% alive at 5 years, (95% CI)".
In the abstract, locate the sentence: "One thousand and three patients were contemporaneously randomised (November 2011 to January 2014)..." and extract the number "1003".

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Synchronous
columns.extraction_plan: While the trial population included a high proportion of newly diagnosed (synchronous) metastatic patients (94% as per Table 1, page 5), the results are not reported by synchronous versus metachronous as distinct prognostic groups. The reporting is for the overall metastatic population and then stratified by "metastatic disease risk group" (low-risk/high-risk), which is a different classification.
columns.column_name_raw: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Synchronous

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Metachronous
columns.extraction_plan: The trial population included relapsing (metachronous) patients (6% as per Table 1, page 5), but the results are not reported by synchronous versus metachronous as distinct prognostic groups. The reporting is for the overall metastatic population and then stratified by "metastatic disease risk group" (low-risk/high-risk), which is a different classification.
columns.column_name_raw: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Metachronous

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | High volume
columns.extraction_plan: The results are reported based on a "high-risk" metastatic disease group, which is defined by criteria commonly associated with high volume/burden of metastases (≥3 bone metastases; visceral metastases; Gleason score ≥8, as stated in the "2.3 Procedures" section on page 4). Table 3 on page 8 presents "Primary and Secondary outcomes, by metastatic disease risk group using LATITUDE criteria" with a dedicated column for "High-risk". The Abstract on page 3 also mentions "metastatic disease risk group: 48% high".
columns.column_name_raw: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | High volume

columns.column_name: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Low volume
columns.extraction_plan: The results are reported based on a "low-risk" metastatic disease group, which represents patients not meeting the criteria for high-risk disease (as defined in the "2.3 Procedures" section on page 4). Table 3 on page 8 presents "Primary and Secondary outcomes, by metastatic disease risk group using LATITUDE criteria" with a dedicated column for "Low-risk". The Abstract on page 3 also mentions "metastatic disease risk group: 44% low".
columns.column_name_raw: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Low volume

columns.column_name: Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: The number and percentage of participants in the treatment arm (SOC + AAP) with high volume of disease (classified as "High-risk" using LATITUDE criteria) is reported in Table 1, under the "SOC + AAP" column, row "High-risk".

columns.column_name: Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Control
columns.extraction_plan: The number and percentage of participants in the control arm (SOC-alone) with high volume of disease (classified as "High-risk" using LATITUDE criteria) is reported in Table 1, under the "SOC-alone" column, row "High-risk".
columns.column_name_raw: Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Control

The trial was conducted in the United Kingdom and Switzerland (page 3, "Funding information"). All author affiliations are from these European countries (pages 1-2). No other regions are mentioned for participant recruitment. Therefore, all participants are considered to be from Europe. The total number of participants in the SOC + AAP (treatment) group was 501.
